name: European TTF Natural Gas
summary: Day 77. **B7 TTF €47.61/MWh MAY 14 (+1.47% 1D, +15% 1M, +34.65% YoY vs pre-war €31.20) — CREEPING UP, SECOND CONSECUTIVE DAILY INCREASE, BUT REMAINS WELL BELOW THE GOLDMAN-ESTIMATED +130% HORMUZ-DISRUPTION TAIL SCENARIO (~€72/MWh).** European-energy channel on neither alarm nor relief. **EIA confirmed earlier this month that international LNG prices have risen amid the Strait of Hormuz closure that has cut around one-fifth of global LNG supply** (markets dossier B7). European gas storage rebuilding ahead of winter remains the tail-risk; the 1.47% daily move is consistent with global-supply-tightness drift rather than acute Hormuz-induced spike. **Trigger 'TTF crosses €70/MWh sustained 5 sessions' NOT ACTIVATED.** Henry Hub US Natural Gas $2.92 (+0.9% 1D, +10.3% 1M, -3.7% 3M). Cheniere (LNG) $241.08 (+0.7% 1D, -5.9% 1M, +10.3% 3M). Structural offsets intact: Hormuz throughput ~30 ships/day per Aramco; Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) still 3-5yr recovery; European inventory refill window tightening into summer. ECB has not characterized Hormuz risk as systemic. Trump-Xi joint statement reduces nominal tail-risk on rhetoric; physical Qatar LNG outage continues to provide structural floor. **B4 TTF €46.63/MWh MAY 13 (-0.11% 1D, basically flat at the €46 range yesterday's brief noted; ~+47% from pre-war €31.20 baseline) — CONFIRMS QUIET; sustained-elevated mode, neither alarm nor relief**. No fresh European Council action priced; Cheniere CCO Hormuz disruption framing carries forward. Container freight FBX13 (China/East Asia → Mediterranean) $4,179/FEU week ending May 12 (-5% w/w) carries forward. European-energy-channel neither escalating nor relieving = sustained-elevated mode. Trigger 'TTF crosses €70/MWh sustained 5 sessions' NOT ACTIVATED. **Henry Hub US Natural Gas $2.89 (+0.8% 1D, +10.6% 1M, -4.8% 3M)** — Hormuz-LNG bid spillover via TTF arbitrage; Cheniere supply pricing pulling US gas higher (~80% crisis). **Cheniere (LNG) $239.38 (-2.0% 1D, -7.6% 1M, +9.6% 3M)** — Q1 $3.5bn hedge loss + LNG infrastructure trade-unwind on Hormuz-tail-risk diminishment + Trump-Xi opening reduces crisis-premium short-term (~85% crisis-trade-unwind). Structural offsets intact: (a) Hormuz throughput 2-5 ships/day per Aramco; (b) Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) still 3-5yr recovery; (c) European inventory refill window tightening into summer. ECB has not characterized Hormuz risk as systemic. PRIOR (May 13): Markets dossier notes ~€44 baseline May 8 → €46.23 May 11 = +5% on news escalation. **PARTIAL DECOUPLING WITH BRENT NARROWING:** Brent +1.3% 1D May 12 vs TTF +4.74% 1D — TTF playing catch-up to crisis pricing. TTF remains BELOW €70+ cascade-intensity threshold; trigger 'TTF crosses €70/MWh sustained 5 sessions' NOT ACTIVATED. **Henry Hub US Natural Gas +0.7% 1D / +11.6% 1M** — indirect Qatar LNG diversion → European TTF demand → US LNG export pull (markets B-section). Cheniere (LNG) $240.70 (+0.5% 1D, -9.1% 1M) — Q1 2026 $3.5B GAAP loss on derivative MtM (long-term LNG hedged at pre-crisis prices); CCO Anatol Feygin: 'astounded that global gas prices have not risen higher with the closure of the Strait of Hormuz disrupting 100 cargoes a month.' US LNG players SHORT the war via hedge books. **Structural offsets intact:** (a) Hormuz throughput DETERIORATED to 2-5 ships/day per Aramco CEO Nasser (vs ~6/day May 10); (b) European inventory refill window tightening into summer (storage at 5-year seasonal low); (c) Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) still 3-5yr recovery. ECB has not characterized Hormuz risk as systemic. **FT framing:** prolonged Hormuz disruption 'could continue to push energy prices higher and reignite inflation pressures' — TTF the most direct read.

current.vs_2022_crisis: still below 2022 crisis levels (€100+); below cascade-intensity threshold €70+; second consecutive daily increase but below Goldman tail-scenario ~€72/MWh

European-energy-channel neither escalating nor relieving. **Henry Hub US Natural Gas $2.89 (+0.8% 1D, +10.6% 1M, -4.8% 3M)** — Hormuz-LNG bid spillover via TTF arbitrage (~80% crisis). **Cheniere (LNG) $239.38 (-2.0% 1D, -7.6% 1M, +9.6% 3M)** = Q1 $3.5bn hedge loss + LNG infrastructure trade-unwind on Hormuz-tail-risk diminishment (~85% crisis-trade-unwind). recent_signals.headline: TTF CRASHED -6.8% MAY 6 TO ~€47.4/MWh ON MOU SCOOP — pricing pause as plausibly durable. -28% est 1M / +35% est 3M. MOU 'both sides lift Hormuz blockades' provision priced Qatar Ras Laffan reopening expectation. TTF still below €70+ cascade-intensity threshold; structural offsets (Hormuz closed, inventory tight, Ras Laffan 3-5yr recovery) intact.
